reason: Response B exhibits hallmarks of Opus's distinctive prose style: it opens with a philosophical reframing of the question itself before engaging the substance, uses recursive self-interruption ("And yet. And yet"), and builds to abstract aphorisms about temporality and mortality that feel characteristically Opus ("We love it the way we love people: because it is mortal and partial and doing its best in conditions that are not entirely fair"). Response A is well-written but follows a more structured, essay-like argumentation pattern — stating a thesis, making the case for each side, then synthesizing — which reads more like a capable but less stylistically distinctive model. B's prose has more of the oblique, ruminative quality Opus tends to favor when given a purely philosophical prompt.
